description: CS Knowledge from Pop Culture & Informal Media
prefix: CS from Pop Culture — real computer science knowledge found in memes, YouTube, movies, games, viral incidents, webcomics, social media, conferences, jokes, and internet culture. Each note should reference specific media (URLs/titles where possible) and explain the actual CS embedded in it.
suffix: Write an encyclopedia-quality knowledge note. Reference specific media (URLs, titles, dates) wherever possible. Explain the real CS concepts embedded in each cultural artifact. Aim for depth: what can a developer actually learn from this?

topics.description: xkcd strips that teach real CS (#327 Bobby Tables, #1205 passwords, #1597 Git, #353 Python, #979 Stack Overflow, #208 regex)
CommitStrip comics on dev patterns: technical debt, scope creep, works-on-my-machine, shipping Friday
Memes that actually teach CS: exit vim, this-is-fine as production monitoring, Drake format for code preferences, expanding brain for algorithms
MonkeyUser.com on the bug lifecycle, code review dynamics, estimation theater, deployment anxiety
Gary Bernhardt's WAT talk, Destroy All Software screencasts, JavaScript type coercion humor that teaches language semantics
James Mickens essays and talks — The Night Watch, The Slow Winter, Life as a Developer; security and systems via comedy
Legendary conference lightning talks: The Birth & Death of JavaScript, Wat, Hammock Driven Development, Simple Made Easy, Are We There Yet
Programmers who teach via comedy — Tom Scott, Fireship 100-seconds, Ben Awad, ThePrimeagen, code review streams

assignments.label: Movies, TV & games with real CS

topics.description: CS accuracy in films: WarGames (social engineering), Hackers, The Social Network, Tron, Sneakers, Office Space (IEEE floating point), The Imitation Game
Mr. Robot technical accuracy — real exploits (Raspberry Pi drops, femtocells, social engineering), why security researchers praised it
Silicon Valley HBO — middle-out compression (real Weissman score), tech startup culture, Pied Piper as distributed web, TechCrunch Disrupt
Games that teach programming: Zachtronics (TIS-100, Shenzhen I/O), Human Resource Machine, Factorio (logistics/optimization), Screeps (JS), SpaceChem
Bugs that went viral: Y2K, Gangnam Style 32-bit overflow, Therac-25, Ariane 5 integer overflow, Knight Capital $440M, 2038 problem, null island
Famous outages as CS lessons: AWS us-east-1, Facebook BGP (Oct 2021), Cloudflare regex, GitLab database deletion, CrowdStrike July 2024
Legendary StackOverflow questions: exit vim (1M+ SO views), components vs elements, why processing sorted array faster, RegEx HTML parsing answer
Falsehoods Programmers Believe series: names, time, addresses, phone numbers, email — real data modeling lessons from crowdsourced edge cases
GitHub README as landing page, awesome lists, profile READMEs, trophy repos, star economy, trending mechanics
Hacker News culture: Show HN, flagging, karma, Paul Graham essays, Rewrite It In Rust meme, Hug of Death, front page dynamics
DevOps via war stories: everything is on fire meme, Friday deploy meme, postmortem culture, SRE via war stories, Sad Mac, BSOD, kernel panic icons
levels.fyi, blind.com, equity vs salary debate, FAANG TC discourse, Wrapped as data viz, microservices architecture debates
Nadia Eghbal Working in Public, core-js plea, colors.js protest, Roads and Bridges report, left-pad incident
Redis/Elastic license changes, MongoDB SSPL, HashiCorp BSL, Open Source Definition wars, copyleft vs permissive debate
Linus Torvalds moments: F YOU NVIDIA, CoC adoption, Tanenbaum debate (monolithic vs micro), kernel design philosophy via flame wars
Hacktoberfest: good (onboarding contributors), bad (2020 spam wave), maintainer revolt, incentive design lessons
LGTM stamp, nitpicking, ship-it vs one-more-thing, PR description art, code review as knowledge transfer
AI hype cycles in pop culture: Copilot copyright debate, ChatGPT launch discourse, AI winter history, Turing test in media, AGI timeline debates
Prompt engineering as cultural phenomenon: jailbreaks, DAN, system prompt leaks, prompt injection as the new SQL injection
Reddit engineering lore: the hug of death, distributed coordination, Digg migration, moderation as governance, subreddit architecture
Twitter/X engineering: Fail Whale, Ruby to JVM migration, snowflake IDs, Elon acquisition chaos, rate limiting drama
YouTube engineering: Gangnam Style 32-bit overflow, recommendation algorithm discourse, Content ID, compression pipeline, Flash to HTML5
Discord engineering: Go to Rust migration blog post, real-time messaging at scale, bot ecosystem as API economy
Esoteric languages as art: Brainfuck, Piet (programs as pixel art), Shakespeare Programming Language, INTERCAL, code golf culture
Demoscene culture: 64K intros, size coding, shader art, Revision/Assembly parties, real optimization techniques via artistic constraint
IOCCC (International Obfuscated C Code Contest), JS1K, code golf — real language mastery via creative constraint
This meeting could have been an email — Agile estimation theater, standup culture, meeting-driven development as anti-pattern
Flash era: Homestar Runner, Newgrounds, ActionScript as first programming language for a generation, death of Flash and web standards victory
Wikipedia/MediaWiki architecture, bot ecosystem, vandalism detection ML, Wikidata, remarkably small team for global scale
TikTok For You Page recommendation discourse, content graph vs social graph, addictiveness debate, ByteDance engineering
